types.description: Strategy to use when merging changesets with conflicting changes.

enumValues.name: LEAVE_CONFLICT_MARKERS
enumValues.description: Let git create conflict markers in files. For modify/delete conflicts, keeps the modified version. Fails on binary conflicts.

enumValues.name: PREFER_OURS
enumValues.description: The conflict is resolved by applying the version of the calling changeset

enumValues.name: PREFER_THEIRS
enumValues.description: The conflict is resolved by applying the version of the other changeset

enumValues.description: Attempt the octopus merge and fail if git merge fails due to conflicts

types.name: Container
types.description: An OCI-compatible container, also known as a Docker container.

fields.name: asService
fields.description:
Turn the container into a Service.

Be sure to set any exposed ports before this conversion.

fields.name: asTarball
fields.description: Package the container state as an OCI image, and return it as a tar archive

args.name: platformVariants
args.description:
Identifiers for other platform specific containers.

Used for multi-platform images.

args.name: forcedCompression
args.description:
Force each layer of the image to use the specified compression algorithm.

If this is unset, then if a layer already has a compressed blob in the engine's cache, that will be used (this can result in a mix of compression algorithms for different layers). If this is unset and a layer has no compressed blob in the engine's cache, then it will be compressed using Gzip.

args.name: mediaTypes
args.description:
Use the specified media types for the image's layers.

Defaults to OCI, which is largely compatible with most recent container runtimes, but Docker may be needed for older runtimes without OCI support.

fields.name: build
fields.description: Initializes this container from a Dockerfile build.

args.name: context
args.description: Directory context used by the Dockerfile.

args.name: dockerfile
args.description: Path to the Dockerfile to use.

args.name: target
args.description: Target build stage to build.

args.name: buildArgs
args.description: Additional build arguments.

args.name: secrets
args.description:
Secrets to pass to the build.

They will be mounted at /run/secrets/[secret-name] in the build container

They can be accessed in the Dockerfile using the "secret" mount type and mount path /run/secrets/[secret-name], e.g. RUN --mount=type=secret,id=my-secret curl [http://example.com?token=$(cat /run/secrets/my-secret)](http://example.com?token=$(cat /run/secrets/my-secret))

args.name: noInit
args.description:
If set, skip the automatic init process injected into containers created by RUN statements.

This should only be used if the user requires that their exec processes be the pid 1 process in the container. Otherwise it may result in unexpected behavior.

fields.name: combinedOutput
fields.description:
The combined buffered standard output and standard error stream of the last executed command

Returns an error if no command was executed

args.name: expand

Force each layer of the exported image to use the specified compression algorithm.

If this is unset, then if a layer already has a compressed blob in the engine's cache, that will be used (this can result in a mix of compression algorithms for different layers). If this is unset and a layer has no compressed blob in the engine's cache, then it will be compressed using Gzip.

args.name: mediaTypes
args.description:
Use the specified media types for the exported image's layers.

Defaults to OCI, which is largely compatible with most recent container runtimes, but Docker may be needed for older runtimes without OCI support.

args.description:
Command to execute. Must be valid exec() arguments, not a shell command. Example: ["go", "run", "main.go"].

To run a shell command, execute the shell and pass the shell command as argument. Example: ["sh", "-c", "ls -l | grep foo"]

Defaults to the container's default arguments (see "defaultArgs" and "withDefaultArgs").

args.description: Apply the OCI entrypoint, if present, by prepending it to the args. Ignored by default.

Skip the automatic init process injected into containers by default.

args.name: cacheKey
args.description:
If set, the given string will be used as the cache key for this secret. This means that any secrets with the same cache key will be considered equivalent in terms of cache lookups, even if they have different URIs or plaintext values.

For example, two secrets with the same cache key provided as secret env vars to other wise equivalent containers will result in the container withExecs hitting the cache for each other.

If not set, the cache key for the secret will be derived from its plaintext value as looked up when the secret is constructed.

If a scheme is specified, a URL is returned. Otherwise, a host:port pair is returned.

fields.description:

Relative paths resolve from the workspace cwd. Absolute paths resolve from the workspace root.

args.name: path
args.description: Location of the directory to retrieve. Relative paths (e.g., "src") resolve from the workspace cwd; absolute paths (e.g., "/src") resolve from the workspace root.

args.description: Exclude artifacts that match the given pattern (e.g., ["node_modules/", ".git*"]).

args.name: include

fields.description:
Returns a File from the workspace.

Relative paths resolve from the workspace cwd. Absolute paths resolve from the workspace root.

args.name: path
args.description: Location of the file to retrieve. Relative paths (e.g., "go.mod") resolve from the workspace cwd; absolute paths (e.g., "/go.mod") resolve from the workspace root.

fields.name: findUp
fields.description:
Search for a file or directory by walking up from the start path within the workspace.

Returns the absolute workspace path if found, or null if not found.

Relative start paths resolve from the workspace cwd.

The search stops at the workspace root and will not traverse above it.

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the file or directory to search for.

args.name: from
args.description: Path to start the search from. Relative paths resolve from the workspace cwd; absolute paths resolve from the workspace root.

args.name: include
args.description: Only include generators matching the specified patterns

fields.description:
Returns a list of files and directories that match the given pattern.

Patterns match paths relative to the workspace root.

fields.name: search
fields.description:
Searches for content matching the given regular expression or literal string.

Uses Rust regex syntax; escape literal ., [, ], {, }, | with backslashes.

Runs ripgrep on the client host, falling back to grep if unavailable.

args.name: paths
args.description: Directory or file paths to search

args.name: globs
args.description: Glob patterns to match (e.g., "*.md")

args.name: pattern
args.description: The text to match.

args.name: literal
args.description: Interpret the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression.

args.name: multiline
args.description: Enable searching across multiple lines.

args.name: dotall
args.description: Allow the . pattern to match newlines in multiline mode.

args.name: insensitive
args.description: Enable case-insensitive matching.

args.name: skipIgnored
args.description: Honor .gitignore, .ignore, and .rgignore files.

args.name: skipHidden
args.description: Skip hidden files (files starting with .).

args.name: filesOnly
args.description: Only return matching files, not lines and content

args.name: limit
args.description: Limit the number of results to return

fields.description: Return all services from modules loaded in the workspace.
